lines.speaker: Garth
lines.text: Hello there. Interested in hearing the tale of Serafina?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes please.

lines.speaker: Garth
lines.text: Great! Now, in case you didn't know, the castle before you is Ver Sinhaza. It's home to the Theatre of Blood.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So it's a form of entertainment for them? Why do the citizens take part?

lines.speaker: Garth
lines.text: The Theatre is run by a famous vampyre noble, Lady Verzik Vitur. She has promised that any citizen of Meiyerditch who survives the Theatre will be freed of the vampyre blood tithes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I suppose that must be rather tempting to a lot of people. So who's this Serafina?

lines.speaker: Garth
lines.text: The Theatre has been around for many years and thousands of people have tried their luck against it. However, in all that time, only one person has ever survived until the end.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Serafina?

lines.speaker: Garth
lines.text: Exactly. Serafina's younger brother was very ill and he wasn't going to survive many more blood tithes. She couldn't just let him die, so she did what no other person ever had.

lines.speaker: Garth
lines.text: She entered the Theatre and succeeded in every challenge she faced. They say that Verzik wasn't too happy about that, but she kept her promise. Serafina and her brother were freed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds like you're a big fan of her.

lines.speaker: Garth
lines.text: Well of course. When she survived the Theatre, she provided hope to every person in Meiyerditch. Hope that they can one day follow in her footsteps.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what happened to her in the end?

lines.speaker: Garth
lines.text: Well I suppose she left Meiyerditch. Can't imagine any free person wanting to stick around here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You suppose? So you don't know for sure?

lines.speaker: Garth
lines.text: Well... No. It was a long time ago. Not many people around who remember it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmmm...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That was Vanstrom Klause and Ranis Drakan!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Safalaan and the Myreque will want to hear about this.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not right now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you need me to do again?

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Go to Ver Sinhaza and see if you can work out what Vanstrom and Ranis are up to. We recently sent a few of King Roald's mercenaries in that direction, talk to them if you need help.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: It's nice to see you again, [player name], and congratulations on your well deserved promotion!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Safalaan! I've seen something I think you should know about.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: What is it, [player name]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I was at Ver Sinhaza and I saw Vanstrom Klause and Ranis Drakan.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: What were they doing?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not sure, they arrived at the same time and both entered the castle.

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: Ver Sinhaza is home to the Theatre of Blood. Maybe they were just there as spectators?

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Hmmm. Ranis is known to visit the Theatre quite regularly. However, I've never heard of Vanstrom going there before. I'd say this is worth investigating.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you need from me?

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Return to Ver Sinhaza and see if you can work out what Vanstrom and Ranis are up to. If you need some help, we recently sent a few of King Roald's mercenaries in that direction.

lines.speaker: Mercenary
lines.text: Hello again. Did you manage to work out what Vanstrom and Ranis are up to?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Mercenary
lines.text: You might be able to spy on them through one of the windows. You'll need to climb to reach them though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Mercenary
lines.text: Hello. You don't look like the usual rabble around here. What's your story?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Safalaan sent me. He said you could help me with something.

lines.speaker: Mercenary
lines.text: Ahh Safalaan. Interesting chap isn't he? Those eyes of his are a bit creepy.

lines.speaker: Mercenary
lines.text: Anyway, we might be able to help but it will need to be quick. We're just about to head into the Theatre. Compared to the citizens, we shouldn't have any issues in there.

lines.speaker: Mercenary
lines.text: So what help do you need?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I saw Vanstrom Klause and Ranis Drakan arriving at the castle earlier. Do you know if they're still here?

lines.speaker: Mercenary
lines.text: Yeah we saw them as well. Pretty hard to miss. We haven't seen them leave yet so I guess they're still inside.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to know what they're doing in there. Any idea how I can do that?

lines.speaker: Mercenary
lines.text: Well this place has quite a few windows. Maybe you could use one of them. They're all high up though so you'd need to climb to reach them.

lines.speaker: Mercenary
lines.text: Aye that might work. Parts of the castle wall have been damaged, it shouldn't be too hard to climb.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Interesting. Thanks for the help.

sections.title: Attempting to climb up the rubble near the bank

lines.speaker: Vyrewatch
lines.text: Get away from there food sack.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Doesn't look like I'll be able to climb up here with that guard watching. I'll need to distract them somehow.

sections.title: Talking to Harper after getting enough coins

lines.speaker: Harpert
lines.text: Do you have my money yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have it here.

lines.speaker: Harpert
lines.text: Hand it over then.

sections.title: Talking to Harper after giving him 1,000 coins

lines.speaker: Harpert
lines.text: Climb the wall when you're ready. I'll distract the guard when you do.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello. I was wondering if you could help me with something.

lines.speaker: Harpert
lines.text: What is it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you think you could distract that guard for me while I climb this wall?

lines.speaker: Harpert
lines.text: I ain't getting on the wrong side of a vyrewatch for nothing. You'll have to pay me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Eugh fine. How much?

lines.speaker: Harpert
lines.text: 1,000 coins should do.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: 1,000 coins? What can you even buy around here for that?

lines.speaker: Harpert
lines.text: There's a guy in sector three who sells weapons. Top secret, the vampyres can't know about it. I want to buy a sword from him so I have a better chance in the Theatre.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fine. I'll come back when I have the money.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fine, here's the money.

lines.speaker: Harpert
lines.text: Thanks. Climb the wall when you're ready. I'll distract the guard when you do.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not paying.

sections.title: Attempting to climb up the rubble near the bank after paying Harpert

lines.speaker: Harpert
lines.text: Hey vampyre! Over here!

lines.speaker: Ranis Drakan
lines.text: ...and you believe this creature will be suitable?

lines.speaker: Verzik Vitur
lines.text: I have no doubt that it will adequately meet your requirements.

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: Perhaps. But I still believe the vyrewatch can handle this.

lines.speaker: Ranis Drakan
lines.text: We've given your vyrewatch more than enough opportunities and yet the Myreque still elude us. It's time for another strategy.

lines.speaker: Verzik Vitur
lines.text: The creature before you has advantages over a vyre. It is the perfect hunter. Once it gets a taste of its foes blood, it can track them anywhere.

lines.speaker: Ranis Drakan
lines.text: We'd still need the blood though.

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: I may have a solution to that, if this is indeed the route we're taking. There is also the question of your sister. She's specifically requested that Safalaan and that outsider be left to her.

lines.speaker: Ranis Drakan
lines.text: Now is not the time for her games. We can't afford to let the enemy slip through our fingers again.

lines.speaker: Ranis Drakan
lines.text: Anyway, you worry about the Myreque. I'll handle Vanescula.

lines.speaker: Ranis Drakan
lines.text: Vanstrom?

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: Hmm?

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: Very well, I will make preparations immediately. The sooner we deal with the Myreque the better. Even our kind have weaknesses and if Serafina could discover them, the Myreque can as well.

lines.speaker: Ranis Drakan
lines.text: Then I think we're done here. Thank you for your time Verzik.

lines.speaker: Verzik Vitur
lines.text: And thank you Ranis. You're always welcome here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well that didn't sound good. I'd better let Safalaan know.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Hello again, [player name]. Do you have any news for us?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I do. It's not good I'm afraid.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I managed to spy on a meeting between Vanstrom, Ranis and Verzik Vitur. Verzik gifted them this strange creature which she said would help them track down the Myreque.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Track down the Myreque? How?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: She claimed that the creature could track anyone provided it got a taste of their blood.

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: So they'd still need blood from one of us? We might be okay then. It's not like the vampyres name and number the tithes they take.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I wouldn't be so sure. Vanstrom seemed to think he had a way around that. Not only that, it sounded like he'd be making his move very soon.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: This isn't good. If Vanstrom thinks he can use this creature against us, I have no reason to disbelieve him. He's proven time and time again that he should not be underestimated.

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: So what's our plan?

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: I'm not sure yet. We'll need to make one quickly though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I help with anything?

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Vertida and I will begin forming a plan of action. In the meantime, do you remember that book on haemalchemy you found for us?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I do.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Well our head researcher, Flaygian Screwte, has been working on translating it. Could you check up on him and see how things are going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Will do.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Remind me.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Following a hint given to you by Sarius Guile, Vanescula Drakan's servant, you found a secret laboratory beneath Meiyerditch. Inside you found a book on haemalchemy.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah yes. So, what's happening with this book?

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Well our head researcher, Flaygian Screwte, has been working on translating it. Could you check up on him and see how things are going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Will do.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there. Safalaan said you were working on translating the haemalchemy book.

lines.speaker: Flaygian Screwte
lines.text: Indeed I am. It's not a quick process I'm afraid. Large parts of the book are written using the vampyric language which isn't easy to translate.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I didn't know the vampyres had their own language.

lines.speaker: Flaygian Screwte
lines.text: Oh yes. It seems to be very old and they don't use it much anymore but it does exist. I suspect it comes in quite useful for things like this where they don't want humans prying into their work.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But you can translate it? How's that going so far?

lines.speaker: Flaygian Screwte
lines.text: Like I say, it's slow. My knowledge on the language only comes from things I've seen around the city so I'm having to make a lot of assumptions. I'm making progress though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Anything to report?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Anything to report?

lines.speaker: Flaygian Screwte
lines.text: The book seems to document a lot of the experiments being performed in that lab you found. Interestingly, the experiments were performed on vampyres themselves, as well as humans.

lines.speaker: Flaygian Screwte
lines.text: It appears that they were trying to use the power of haemalchemy to modify both humans and vampyres.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is haemalchemy?

lines.speaker: Flaygian Screwte
lines.text: There are some that believe there is a hidden power in blood. Haemalchemy is the act of modifying blood, both magically and scientifically, to harness this power.

lines.speaker: Flaygian Screwte
lines.text: It seems that the vampyres are strong believers in the power of haemalchemy as they've been experimenting quite heavily.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why?

lines.speaker: Flaygian Screwte
lines.text: It looks like they had two reasons. First of all they were looking for a way to increase the amount of blood that can be harvested from a human.

lines.speaker: Flaygian Screwte
lines.text: Unsurprisingly, the vampyres here have multiplied much faster than the humans. Perhaps there's no longer enough blood to go around.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And the other reason?

lines.speaker: Flaygian Screwte
lines.text: That's the more interesting one. As we're already aware, vampyres possess some form of immunity against our attacks.

lines.speaker: Flaygian Screwte
lines.text: It starts off as only partial in juveniles and juvinates but by the time they become vyres they are totally invulnerable.

lines.speaker: Flaygian Screwte
lines.text: This book implies that the vampyres immunity isn't perfect and that it can be overcome. It seems they were trying to improve this immunity.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Overcome? How?

lines.speaker: Flaygian Screwte
lines.text: I haven't got that far yet I'm afraid.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmmm. I might actually be able to help with that bit. I was spying on Vanstrom and Ranis earlier for Safalaan and I heard Vanstrom say something quite interesting.

lines.speaker: Flaygian Screwte
lines.text: What did he say?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He implied that Serafina had discovered their weakness.

lines.speaker: Flaygian Screwte
lines.text: Serafina? As in the Serafina who survived the Theatre of Blood?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I believe so.

lines.speaker: Flaygian Screwte
lines.text: That would actually make a lot of sense. We've always wondered how Serafina managed to survive all of the challenges in the Theatre.

lines.speaker: Flaygian Screwte
lines.text: But what if she never needed to? Maybe she took advantage of the vampyres weakness and forced them to let her leave?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you think this is worth exploring?

lines.speaker: Flaygian Screwte
lines.text: I do. I'll stay here and keep working on this book. You head out and see if you can find out what Serafina discovered. Safalaan should be able to help you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do I need to do again?

lines.speaker: Flaygian Screwte
lines.text: We need to find out what Serafina discovered about the vampyres. Talk to Safalaan, he should be able to help you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How can I find the house again?

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: You'll find it in sector four. To get there, leave the base, head north and then head west. Go all the way to the western wall and you'll find a broken fountain. The house is the one directly south of the fountain.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi Safalaan. How are things going?

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Not good I'm afraid, [player name]. We've looked over our options and we can't see any solution to this problem. If the vampyres truly have the ability to track us then we won't be safe anywhere in Morytania.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what do we do?

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: We'll have to leave. We should be able to sneak out of Meiyerditch using the same route you did.

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: We can meet up with Veliaf and the rest of the Myreque in Burgh de Rott. Once we're all together, we can travel to Paterdomus and cross the Salve into Misthalin.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Retreat to Misthalin? But what about all the people here that need our help? They'll die without us.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: I know it's not ideal, but we can't help anyone if we're dead. The only place we'll be safe from this new threat is on the other side of the Salve.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There must be another way. What about Roald's mercenaries? Can't they help us?

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Even with their help the vampyres outnumber us ten to one. The only reason we've survived this long is by hiding in the shadows. Once the vampyres know where we are, we'll be doomed.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: I'm sorry, [player name]. This isn't the end of our fight though. Once we're in Misthalin we can build up our strength and find more support. We'll come back stronger than ever before.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So when do we leave?

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: It will take a while to pack up all of our equipment but we aim to be gone as soon as possible.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you think there's time for one last job before we go?

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: I guess that depends what it is.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: While translating the haemalchemy book, Flaygian found reference to a possible weakness in the vampyres.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not only that, I also heard Vanstrom talking about this same weakness. He implied that Serafina found a way to take advantage of it.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Serafina? I suspect she's nothing more than a myth. A tale fabricated by the vampyres to drive people to that Theatre of theirs.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But this came from Vanstrom himself.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: That is a valid point I guess. Old Man Ral used to tell plenty of stories about Serafina and he did once tell me where she apparently lived.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: I suppose we have time to go and have a quick look around while the others pack everything up here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So where do we go?

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: You'll find the house in sector four. To get there, leave the base, head north and then head west. Go all the way to the western wall and you'll find a broken fountain. The house is the one directly south of the fountain.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: It's best that we travel separately so you head out first and I'll meet you there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This looks like the fountain that Safalaan mentioned. Serafina's house must be this one to the south.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: So this is where Serafina lived. Or so they claim.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Everyone else I've spoken to looks up to Serafina, but you don't even think she was real.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: I guess it's not about if she was real or not. It's about what she is now. A symbol. A false one.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Every day, citizens of Meiyerditch throw their lives away in that damn Theatre. All because of her.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But why don't they fight with the Myreque instead?

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: I guess they don't think the vampyres will ever be beaten. They believe that joining the Myreque will lead to certain death.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Thanks to Serafina, they believe that they at least have a chance with the Theatre.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Anyway, I'm getting distracted. There's a locked door here that might be hiding what we're looking for. Let's see if we can find a way to open it.

sections.title: Talking to Safalaan again before finding any ingredients or checking the door

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Found anything yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: We should keep looking.

sections.title: Talking to Safalaan again after finding the Mysterious herb but before checking the door

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Found anything yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I found a herb. It's not a type I recognise.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Maybe a potion is needed. See if you can make one. You can get some water from the broken fountain outside.

sections.title: Talking to Safalaan again after finding the Mysterious meat but before checking the door

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Found anything yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I found a herb. It's not a type I recognise.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: How odd. We should see if we can find anything else.

sections.title: Talking to Safalaan again after making the unfinished potion but before checking the door

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Found anything yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I found some ingredients and started making a potion with them.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: A potion? See if you can finish it off.

sections.title: Talking to Safalaan again after making the potion but before checking the door

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Found anything yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I found some ingredients and made a potion with them.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Interesting. Maybe there's something you can use it with.

sections.title: Talking to Safalaan again after checking the door but before using the potion on it

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Found anything yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The door has an inscription on it.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: What does it say?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: 'Blood is the price of victory.'

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Blood? Maybe haemalchemy is involved here. We should keep looking around.

sections.title: Talking to Safalaan again after using the potion on the door

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Found anything yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I found some ingredients for a potion. I made it and tried using it with the door but nothing happened. I also found an inscription on the door.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: What does it say?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: 'Blood is the price of victory.'

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Hmm... I wonder...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are you thinking?

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: I have an idea that may work. Bring me an empty vial.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are you doing?

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: 'Blood is the price of victory.' Now try making the potion with this. If my suspicions are correct it should work.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fine. Next time please warn me before you start cutting yourself.

sections.title: Talking to Safalaan again if the player did not have an empty vial

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Do you have that vial?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Well bring one to me. I have an idea.

sections.title: Talking to Safalaan again after getting the Vial of blood

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could I have another vial of blood.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: I don't have unlimited blood you know! Use the one you've got.

sections.title: Talking to Safalaan again after losing the Vial of blood

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could I have another vial of blood.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: I hope you don't think I have unlimited blood to spare. Bring me another vial and try not to lose this one.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Please be more careful with this one. I'd rather not die of blood loss.

sections.title: Talking to Safalaan again if the blood potion has been partially made

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Any luck with that potion?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm still working on it.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Keep at it.

sections.title: Talking to Safalaan again if the blood potion has been made but not used on the door

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Any luck with that potion?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've got a finished potion here.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Well try using it with the door. If my suspicions are correct it should work.

sections.title: Talking to Safalaan again if the blood potion has been made and was used on the door

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Any luck with that potion?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It worked this time. The door is unlocked.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Good work. Hopefully there's something useful behind it.

sections.title: Talking to Safalaan after obtaining the old notes
Talking to Safalaan after giving him the old notes

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: I think we're all done here. We should head back to the hideout.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Any luck?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I found some old notes behind the door.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Excellent. Hopefully they contain something useful. We should head back to the hideout and give them to Flaygian. I can take them if you want?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here you go.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Thanks. We should head back to the hideout.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll look after them.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: As you wish. Just make sure they get to Flaygian at the hideout.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: How are things looking Vertida?

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: All of the essentials are packed up and ready to go.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Excellent. Let the others know that we'll be leaving shortly.

lines.speaker: Flaygian Screwte
lines.text: I... I don't understand...

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: What is it, Flaygian?

lines.speaker: Flaygian Screwte
lines.text: I'm looking through these notes you found but there's nothing here.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: What do you mean nothing?

lines.speaker: Flaygian Screwte
lines.text: As in nothing. It's just a load of rubbish. Why would Serafina have written this?

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Because she might not have been the one to write it. [player name], what was it that Verzik said about this creature Vanstrom is using?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That it can track anyone if it has a taste of their blood.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: And when we were at Serafina's home, we used our blood to open that door.

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: You don't mean...

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Vanstrom! He used us as bait. Vertida, get the others. We need to leave now!

lines.speaker: Mekritus A'hara
lines.text: Oh f...

lines.speaker: Kael Forshaw
lines.text: Mekritus!

lines.speaker: Andiess Juip
lines.text: Time to die beast!

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: Flaygian look out!

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: No! Flaygian!

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: What's happening? Safalaan?

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: It's weakened! Let's finish it off!

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: Safalaan! Are you okay?

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: I'll be fine. Now we need to get out of here before Vanstrom and his vampyres turn up.

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: [player name], we have an old hideout beneath Old Man Ral's house in southern Meiyerditch. Meet us there.

sections.title: Talking to Veliaf before talking to Vertida

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Well met!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Veliaf.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Anything to report?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Vanstrom attacked the Myreque in Meiyerditch!

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Well we need to go help!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The attack is already over. We won but there were casualties.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Damn them vampyres. We need to find a way to turn things in our favour.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Mekritus... Andiess... Flaygian... How many more need to die to those monsters?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you okay, Safalaan?

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: I knew Serafina was a lie. But I wanted so hard to believe. To believe that someone could have actually beaten those vampyres.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: But I was naive and people died because of it.

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: More would have died if not for you. Your magic weakened that thing. We would never have been able to kill it without that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What was that magic?

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: To be honest, I don't know. It's a power I've had for as long as I can remember. I've never been able to control it though, it only seems to come on when I'm angry or threatened.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How strange.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Indeed. I wish I could tell you more about it but I don't actually remember much about the times it has happened. It's like I have these blank spots in my memory.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Anyway, now is not the time to dwell on it.

lines.speaker: Kael Forshaw
lines.text: So what's the plan boss?

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: We should make our way to Burgh de Rott and meet up with Veliaf and the others.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You mean we're still leaving?

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: What other choice do we have?

lines.speaker: Kael Forshaw
lines.text: I just lost some very good friends of mine. I can't walk away from that. The vampyres need to pay.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: We all nearly died back there. I don't intend to throw any more lives away today. We are leaving.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Just think for a second though. The vampyres will probably think we all died in the attack. They'll have dropped their guard. Now is the best time to retaliate.

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: That is a good point.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: It is. However, in case you'd forgotten, we don't have anything to retaliate with. Serafina was a lie, just another one of Vanstrom's little plots.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: We have no weapon to use against the vampyres. And with Flaygian gone, that won't be changing.

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: Actually, I think you might be wrong there.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: Flaygian managed to translate some more of the haemalchemy book while you two were investigating Serafina.

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: We never got the chance to look over his new discoveries before the attack. However, I did recover his notes as we left.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Hmmm... All right. [player name], work with Vertida and see if you can finish off Flaygian's work. Make no mistake though, if this doesn't come to anything then we will be leaving.

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: Do you need any help with Flaygian's notes?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need the Rod of Ivandis

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: I imagine you'd know more about that than me. Weren't you the one who remade it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I was but I gave it to Veliaf.

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: Well I'm sure he still has it. You should ask him if you can have it back. Alternatively, you could always make another.

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: Do you need anything else?

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: Check the crates around here, we should have some chain lying around.

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: Do you need anything else?

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: Well we have some blessed silver sickles in storage here. You should check the crates. What kind of enchantment does it need?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: An emerald enchantment

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: Well you'll need to get an emerald and attach it to the sickle. You'll be needing a chisel to do that. Once you've attached the emerald, you should be able to enchant the sickle using an enchantment spell.

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: Do you need anything else?

sections.title: After reading creating the Ivandis Flail

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Vertida. I've finished the weapon.

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: Great work. You should let Safalaan know.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Vertida.

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: Hello there. I have these notes for us to review. Come back when you have room for them and we'll get started.

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: Hello there. Here are the notes Flaygian made. Have a read through and let me know if you need any help.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Well met!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Veliaf.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Anything to report?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I was wondering if I could ask a favour?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Of cause, ask away.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Would it be possible for me to have the Rod of Ivandis back?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: What do you need it for?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I want to convert it into a flail. I have reason to believe that will make it even more effective against vampyres.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Well I can hardly say no to that. Here you go.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Well I'd be happy to help but you don't have enough room for it.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Looks like you have one right there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh yeah, how didn't I notice.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: But I already gave it to you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I lost it.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Well you'll just have to make another then. That was the only one I had.

lines.speaker: Kael Forshaw
lines.text: Hopefully Flaygain's work will help us against the vampyres.

sections.title: Talking to Safalaan after creating the Ivandis flail

lines.speaker: Kael Forshaw
lines.text: Hello, player. I'll be leaving for Ver Sinhaza soon. You should meet me there.

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: Hello, player. You'd better get moving. Ranis will be starting his speech soon.

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: Well I have made a few more. I suppose I could let you have one. I'll have to charge you though, some of this stuff isn't easy to get hold of.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How much?

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: 20,000 coins should do it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds good to me. I'll take it.

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: Here you go. Put it to good use

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: I'm afraid you don't have enough room for it. Come back when you do.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's a bit too much for me. I guess I'll make myself another one instead.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't have that much on me.

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: I'll be here when you do.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good news Safalaan, the weapon is complete.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: About time we had some good news. Great work.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do we do now?

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Well I wasn't convinced at first but it turns out you were right. The vampyres seem to believe we're all dead. That's not all though, tell them Kael.

lines.speaker: Kael Forshaw
lines.text: Ranis Drakan himself has sent an open invitation to the people of Meiyerditch, inviting them to Ver Sinhaza.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How come?

lines.speaker: Kael Forshaw
lines.text: Apparently he intends to officially announce the defeat of the Myreque.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: This is quite possibly one of the best opportunities we've ever been given. Ranis Drakan is brother to Lowerniel Drakan himself and is one of the most senior figures in vampyric society.

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: And for the first time, we have a weapon that could actually defeat him.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Exactly. This is our chance to bring real hope to these people. The death of any vampyre would send a strong message but the death of Ranis Drakan could change everything.

lines.speaker: Kael Forshaw
lines.text: You don't need to convince me. It's time to show these monsters that their days are done.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: [player name], you are not a citizen of Meiyerditch. You have never felt the pain that these people have. And yet, you have fought for them all the same.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Now I must ask that you fight for them again. You are the most experienced amongst us. You have the best chance of defeating Ranis.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've come this far. I'm not about to leave now. What's our plan.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Take the new weapon and head to Ver Sinhaza. Kael will meet you there. Wait until Ranis has begun his speech, that will be the best time to strike.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I won't let you down.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Thank you, [player name]. Myreque prevail.

lines.speaker: Kael Forshaw
lines.text: Well met, [player name]. Ranis will be starting his speech soon. Are you ready?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm ready.

lines.speaker: Kael Forshaw
lines.text: Very well. Good luck.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Kael Forshaw
lines.text: Well just let me know when you are.

lines.speaker: Ranis Drakan
lines.text: Citizens of Meiyerditch! I stand before you today so that we may share in a moment of triumph!

lines.speaker: Ranis Drakan
lines.text: For years, the Myreque have lurked in the shadows of this city! Their acts of defiance tainting the greatness that we have all achieved together!

lines.speaker: Ranis Drakan
lines.text: Today, that defiance finally came to an end! Today, we entered the home of the Myreque and eradicated their kind!

lines.speaker: Ranis Drakan
lines.text: Our efforts today would not have been possible without every single one of you. The blood you provide ensures that we remain ever capable of facing these threats.

lines.speaker: Ranis Drakan
lines.text: For that, you should all be proud.

lines.speaker: Ranis Drakan
lines.text: However, while today is very much a day of celebration, it should also act as a reminder.

lines.speaker: Ranis Drakan
lines.text: Rebellion. Resistance. Defiance. These are not the path to freedom. They are merely the path to death.

lines.speaker: Ranis Drakan
lines.text: Anyone who wishes to be free of this life should instead look to Serafina.

lines.speaker: Ranis Drakan
lines.text: Follow in her footsteps. Enter the Theatre and survive. Do this and freedom will be yours.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: As good as all of that sounds, I do have just a few issues with it.

lines.speaker: Ranis Drakan
lines.text: What is this?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You stand here claiming the Myreque are dead but I'm here to prove otherwise.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: For too long you have lorded yourself over these people. Today, that comes to an end. Today...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ...you die.

lines.speaker: Ranis Drakan
lines.text: You know, over the course of my life, I have tasted more blood than you could ever imagine. The variety of flavours is incredible, and yet, there's one taste that all others pale in comparison to.

lines.speaker: Ranis Drakan
lines.text: You see, there's nothing sweeter than a taste of hope. And you're full of it.

lines.speaker: Ranis Drakan
lines.text: I'm going to enjoy killing you.

lines.speaker: Ranis Drakan
lines.text: I take it you killed that abomination then? It was a human once you know. How does that feel?

lines.speaker: Ranis Drakan
lines.text: He was doomed. With his illness he'd have soon died with or without our tithing. We saved him, just like she wanted. All for you to just kill him!

lines.speaker: Ranis Drakan
lines.text: She was one of the smart ones. She was willing! Saving him was all that mattered. She was ready to sacrifice anything for that.

lines.speaker: Ranis Drakan
lines.text: It's a shame her mind is no longer there. One of the negatives of the process unfortunately. Otherwise I'd have introduced you. I'm sure she'd have loved to meet her brother's killer.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sooo... I guess this is the part where I say something...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: For hundreds of years, the people of Morytania have suffered under Lord Lowerniel Drakan and his vampyres.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: For too long, they've filled you with false hope. Hope of freedom. All you have to do is follow Serafina and you'll be free. Or so they claim.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well whoever Serafina was, she is gone. There is no freedom in that Theatre. No hope. Just lies. If freedom is truly what you want, there is another path.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You see, the vampyres are not as powerful as they'd have you believe. Ranis Drakan lies dead before you. Killed by a mere human. If he can die, so can the rest.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So now, I ask that you join us. Fight alongside the Myreque and together, we will push back the darkness of Hallowvale.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Together, we will end the tyranny of the Drakan family. Together, we will bring hope to all of Morytania.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Myreque prevail!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good news Safalaan, Ranis Drakan is no more!

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: I've already heard. The whole city is talking about it.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: [player name], it can never be understated what you have done today. This is the turning of the tide in our battle and it's all down to you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks Safalaan. What happens now?

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Lowerniel Drakan is unlikely to ignore the death of his brother. He will do everything he can to destroy us. I don't expect things to be easy for us from here.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: However, thanks to you, we should hopefully see more support for our own cause. You've finally given these people something to fight for. Something real.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: We'll start planning what our next steps should be right away. You can leave that to us though, you've earnt a break.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Also, it seems only right that you are rewarded for what you've done today. I can't offer much but I do have this tome of knowledge that might come in useful. I think Kael has something for you as well.

lines.speaker: Kael Forshaw
lines.text: That's right. I recovered this medallion from Ranis, you might find a good use for it.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Anyway, we'll let you get off. And once again, [player name], thank you.

lines.speaker: Kael Forshaw
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Kael Forshaw
lines.text: Of course.

lines.speaker: Kael Forshaw
lines.text: Much better thanks to you. It actually feels like we're winning for once.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Glad to hear it.

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: Good to have you here, player.Everthing seems to feel more positive when you're around.

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: Hmmm... Lord Drakan won't be pleased about this.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Oh I'm counting on it...

lines.speaker: Kael Forshaw
lines.text: That was amazing, [player name]. Now you'd better get back to the hideout. This place will be filled with vampyres soon.
